title: ProcScript Module for breakpoints
html:
ProcScript Module for breakpoints

  • Last UpdatedMai 03, 2023
    • Uniface
    • 10.4

Name of the ProcScript module.

For a new breakpoint, enter the name of the ProcScript module in which to set the breakpoint. The name must have a format suitable for the module type:

  • Operations—h OperationName
  • Form triggers—i FormTriggerAbbreviation
  • Other triggers—u n \ TriggerAbbreviation, where u indicates trigger in the field script container and n is a sequential number assigned by the ProcScript compiler.
  • Global ProcScript modules—c ModuleName
  • Local ProcScript modules—m ModuleName
Table 1. List of all module indicator letters and their description
Module indicators Description
f Indicates operation in an application script container.
g Indicates trigger in an application script container.
h Indicates operation in a component script container.
i Indicates trigger in a component script container.
j Indicates operation in a collection script container.
k Indicates trigger in a collection script container.
q Indicates operation in an occurrence script container.
r Indicates trigger in an occurrence script container.
s Indicates operation in a field script container.
u Indicates trigger in a field script container.
m Indicates a module of type entry.
n Indicates a module of type function. If a function is defined at entity or field level, it is followed by a number and a back slash, where the number is a sequential number assigned by the ProcScript compiler.
